summary: Frontend-only verification of Print Request enhancements (A) terminal fallback for stale state_machine_id, (B) live CDR file link section on print request detail, (C) confirmation checkbox gating the Create Print Request submit, and (D) CEO delete flow on the print-requests list. All four behaviors work as specified. Fixed one incidental UI bug found during testing: an orphan ')}' rendered as literal text near Notes/Print Specs on PrintRequestDetail.js.

ui_bugs.issue: Orphan ')}' from a removed conditional wrapper was rendering as literal text between the CDR card and the Notes/Print Specs card (visible as ')}' after 'Matte finish'). Fixed by re-wrapping the Notes/Print Specs card in {pr.notes && (...)} conditional.

test_results.terminal_fix_print_request_button: PASS - On lead 1b5f9703 (Sneha Reddy) Customer Branding section shows DRN-MIGTEST-001 (Production Completed) with a solid emerald button data-testid=create-print-request-btn-TESTMIG-42dff3ce (bg rgb(5,150,105), white text). Button is visible and clicking opens the Create Print Request dialog.
test_results.confirmation_checkbox_gating: PASS - Dialog title 'Create Print Request For DRN-MIGTEST-001 · Bottle Design' opens. pr-submit-btn has the disabled attribute present ('') before checking. The checkbox pr-confirm-checkbox with label 'I confirm that this is not a sample request or a design request. This request is for the customer’s initial order.' toggles the submit button to enabled (el.disabled === false, disabled attr removed).
test_results.cdr_live_link_section: PASS - /print-requests/e2aa6a51-682c-462c-883e-6daad14d555e renders data-testid=print-cdr-section titled 'CDR File Link for Printing' with subtitle 'Always reflects the current File Link on the source design request.' Two links present: print-cdr-link-0 href=https://cdr.example.com/final.cdr and print-cdr-link-1 href=https://drive.google.com/cdr-test-123.
test_results.ceo_delete_from_list: PASS - CEO sees 5 row-level trash buttons (print-delete-<id>) on /print-requests desktop table (5 visible + 5 mobile duplicates hidden). Clicking one opens print-delete-dialog ('Delete print request? This will permanently delete PR-2026-0005. This action cannot be undone.'), with print-delete-confirm and print-delete-cancel present. Cancel closes the dialog and no request is deleted. (Actual DELETE not executed to preserve data per request.)

action_items: Optional: review the two extra print-delete-* nodes in the DOM (mobile+desktop render both) — not a bug (mobile is hidden), just a minor duplicate testid concern for automation.
critical_code_review_comments: PrintRequestDetail.js had an orphan ')}' due to a stray closing of a conditional that no longer existed — fixed. Root cause: when the CDR section was added, the wrapping '{pr.notes && (' opener was removed but the ')}' closer stayed. This kind of leftover is easy to miss because JSX still parses (JS-in-JSX only matches braced expressions).
Both desktop and mobile layouts share the same data-testid for row delete buttons (print-delete-<id>). Consider suffixing '-mobile' on the mobile row to avoid ambiguous selectors in automated tests.
seed_data_creation: None (used pre-seeded DRN-MIGTEST-001 and PR-2026-0001 as instructed).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Print Request testids: create-print-request-btn-<designReqId>, pr-submit-btn, pr-cancel-btn, pr-confirm-checkbox (Create Print Request dialog). PrintRequestDetail page: print-cdr-section, print-cdr-link-0..N, print-cdr-empty (when no links). List: print-delete-<prId>, print-delete-dialog, print-delete-confirm, print-delete-cancel. CEO can see delete buttons on all 5 rows.
rca of the issue: N/A for the four features (all working). For the orphan ')}': the closing brace of a previously-existing '{pr.notes && (...)}' wrapper around Notes/Print Specs card was left behind after a refactor that inserted the CDR card just above. React rendered '}' and ')' as JSX text children of the parent div. Reproduction was: visit any Print Request detail page — the two characters appeared between the Notes card and the enclosing div's close. Mitigation applied: re-wrapped Notes card in '{pr.notes && (...)}' matching the orphaned close.
